title: Media Representatives

narrative.executive_view: Media Representatives is a narrow, heterogeneous commission business: the Census definition is independent sale of media time or space for media owners, rather than an ad agency or a media-buying shop [S1]. The buy thesis is weak. AI can remove proposal, research, traffic and billing work, but its main economic consequence may be to make the owner’s represented inventory easier to sell through programmatic channels rather than to create a scarce new service. Katz, the large national incumbent, earns commissions on national spot and online advertising sold for station clients [S2]; that confirms both the commission model and the dependence on a media-owner client relationship. I classify terminal demand as PRESSURED rather than MELTING because the NAICS boundary includes online and specialty representation, but a buyer should underwrite a low multiple, contract-transfer risk, and a much smaller target universe than total establishments suggest.
narrative.how_ai_changes_work: The supplied 5418 occupation mix is an imperfect proxy, so the scorecard does not treat its PR and creative shares as literal media-rep staffing. In an actual representative, AI is most useful in audience research, RFP responses, proposal assembly, avails packaging, order entry, trafficking, invoicing and reconciliation; relationship selling to stations, publishers and agency accounts remains the core human work. This is consistent with broader adoption being early rather than saturated: Census BTOS reported 17–20% of firms using AI in business functions from December 2025 through May 2026 and less than 20% for firms with four or fewer employees [S3]. The selected replaceable share is therefore a task estimate, not a claim that the code’s sales function disappears.
narrative.value_capture: The operator charges through a commission structure, not a time-and-materials fee, so labor savings from proposal and traffic automation can initially remain with the representative [S2]. That favorable form is offset by weak bargaining power: the represented media owner can renegotiate commissions, sell more inventory directly, or expose inventory to programmatic channels. Comscore reported that CTV reached 28% of surveyed media budgets in 2025 and that nearly half of marketers increasing programmatic CTV budgets were reallocating from linear TV [S4]. This is a distinct capture channel from labor automation: it threatens the commission base and the owner’s relationship moat, not the task-share estimate.
narrative.adoption_timing: I use 20% current effective adoption and four years to 50% effective adoption. BTOS is not a media-representative survey and measures any-business-function use, so it cannot prove workflow-level automation in this code [S3]. It does, however, establish that broad small-business adoption has not already crossed the 50% boundary. The four-year estimate assumes faster adoption in documents, research and order workflows than the all-business average, tempered by fragmented small firms, contract-specific traffic systems, and continuing human sales relationships.
narrative.consolidation_economics: National representation is already concentrated: iHeart describes Katz as a media representation firm earning commissions from station advertising [S2]. That is an incumbent benchmark, not evidence that there is an active, acquirable roll-up lane among the 50 deterministic $1–10M EBITDA targets. The closest public lower-middle-market valuation proxy is media buying agencies, where the cited marketplace describes 4.5–6.0x EBITDA for well-run agencies and specifically warns that value is on net fee revenue rather than gross media spend [S5]. A small representative with terminable media-owner contracts and declining linear exposure deserves a discount to that proxy; the selected 3.5x buy and 3.75x exit are deliberately conservative estimates, not observed 541840 transaction multiples.
narrative.terminal_demand: Paid demand survives in niches where a representative owns a useful local, political, specialty or digital selling relationship, but the code’s overall terminal is pressured. The Census scope expressly includes independent representatives selling time or space for media owners [S1], and iHeart’s filing shows the model still generates commission revenue [S2]. At the same time, digital advertising reached nearly $300 billion in 2025, up 13.9%, while programmatic CTV budgets were moving from linear TV [S4, S6]. AI makes the transaction more automatable; it does not create the represented media inventory or guarantee that a third-party rep retains the sale.
narrative.risks_and_uncertainty: The central uncertainty is composition. NAICS 541840 includes radio, television, print, internet and other media representatives, while the public evidence largely describes a large broadcast incumbent and broad advertising markets [S1, S2]. The frozen role mix is at four-digit 5418 and materially contaminated by agency, PR and creative occupations, so actual role fractions and AI value could move sharply. There is no public, code-specific lower-middle-market transaction database or owner-age census in the sources reopened here. Multiples, ownership age, succession and active-consolidator inputs are accordingly low-confidence estimates with explicit proxy caveats; no investment decision should rely on them without a target-level contract, client-concentration and renewal review.

sources.title: North American Industry Classification System — 541840 Media Representatives
sources.what_it_supports: Official scope: independent representatives selling media time or space for media owners; distinguishes 541840 from agencies and media buying.

sources.title: iHeartMedia 2025 Form 10-K
sources.what_it_supports: Katz Media earns commissions from national spot and online advertising sold for represented radio and television clients; documents the incumbent commission model.

sources.title: Large Firms With at Least 20 Employees Biggest AI Users
sources.what_it_supports: BTOS broad-business AI-use results: 17–20% overall use in the December 2025–May 2026 period and lower use for the smallest firms.

sources.title: Proximic by Comscore Releases 2025 State of Programmatic Report
sources.what_it_supports: Reported CTV share of surveyed media budgets and reallocations from linear TV, supporting the programmatic/linear-transition risk.

sources.title: Media Buying Agency Valuation
sources.what_it_supports: Public marketplace proxy: well-run media-buying agencies typically 4.5–6.0x EBITDA on fee revenue, with lower values for weak contracts, concentration or missing programmatic capability.

sources.title: IAB/PwC Internet Advertising Revenue Report: Full Year 2025
sources.what_it_supports: IAB reports nearly $300 billion of U.S. digital advertising revenue in 2025 and 13.9% year-over-year growth; broad advertising-market context only.

ai_replaceable_share.rationale: AI can automate research, proposal, traffic, billing and administrative tasks, while station/publisher and agency relationship selling remains human. The supplied 4-digit role proxy is reconstructed task-by-task and carries a contamination caveat.
caveats: The supplied role mix is 4-digit 5418, not 541840, and includes PR, creative and display occupations that are not a literal media-representative workforce.
Programmatic substitution is excluded from this V estimate and addressed in capture and terminal demand.

role_judgments.rationale: Prospecting and proposal drafting automate, but negotiated account selling persists.
Proxy PR/deck work is substantially automatable, though this occupation is scope-contaminated.
Audience research, reporting and proposal inputs are directly assistable.
Internal reporting automates but client and media-owner management remains.
Sales collateral and layout are highly AI-assistable; proxy role caveat applies.
Promotion support can be automated in part but is not a core code role.
Service-sales administration automates, relationship selling does not.
Proxy display-production work has automatable preparation tasks.
Planning and reporting automate while commercial leadership persists.
Creative review is assisted, with judgment retained; proxy role caveat applies.
Residual is assumed to contain traffic, billing, operations and unlisted sales support; this is a bounded judgment.

pi_dist.rationale: Commission pricing permits initial retention of labor savings, but represented media owners and programmatic channels can pressure the commission base.
caveats: No public source measures 541840 commission-rate changes after AI adoption.

pi_moat.rationale: Exclusive media-owner contracts and sales relationships can matter, but the product is an intermediary service exposed to direct and programmatic routes.
caveats: Contract term, transferability and client concentration are target-specific and unobserved.

t50_years.rationale: Current broad adoption remains below 50%; document-heavy selling and programmatic tooling speed deployment, but small-firm fragmentation and relationship workflows slow effective adoption.
caveats: BTOS is broad-business evidence, not a 541840 workflow survey.

current_adoption_pct.rationale: Uses the upper end of BTOS’s 17–20% all-business use range as a conservative proxy for current effective AI use in this document-intensive code.
caveats: This is an all-business proxy, not an industry measurement.

historical_analogs.value: Programmatic CTV budget reallocation from linear TV is an analogue for automated inventory transactions displacing labor-intensive selling, but it is not a measured 541840 adoption curve.
historical_analogs.rationale: Comscore documents a relevant channel-transition mechanism rather than a direct analogue for this code.
historical_analogs.plausible_range: Transition could be faster in digital inventory and slower in local or specialty sales.
caveats: Not used to set V; used only to frame adoption and terminal uncertainty.

selection.rationale: A 40% working estimate is used because no code-specific owner-age dataset was found; it is not a reported statistic.
caveats: S5 is a media-buying valuation proxy and does not report owner age for 541840.

succession_shortage_documented.rationale: No reopened source documents a 541840 succession shortage, so this input is conservatively false.
caveats: False means not documented in reopened sources, not that succession risk cannot exist at a target.

active_consolidators.rationale: Katz is a large incumbent representative, but the reopened filing does not document a current acquisition program for independent 541840 targets; count is conservatively zero.
caveats: This is not a complete private-deal search and does not measure historic consolidation.

buy_mult.rationale: Selected below the 4.5–6.0x media-buying-agency proxy because 541840 commission contracts and linear-media exposure are less durable than a well-run agency’s fee base.
caveats: There is no reopened 541840 transaction-comps dataset; S5 is a media-buying proxy, not an exact NAICS comparable.

exit_mult.rationale: A modest 0.25x improvement assumes a buyer can document diversified digital/specialty inventory and transferable contracts; programmatic migration limits upside.
caveats: This is a target-execution assumption rather than observed exit evidence; no multiple expansion is used in C or terminal demand.

pricing_structure.value: Commission revenue on media sold for represented media owners
pricing_structure.rationale: iHeart’s 2025 filing describes Katz’s contractual commissions from national spot and online advertising sold for station clients.
pricing_structure.plausible_range: Commission structure; target commission rate and contract terms vary
caveats: The observed source is a large incumbent, not a representative sample of all 541840 firms.

cross_checks.uplift_vs_A_consistent: A roughly 40% task-share estimate is meaningful but does not imply immediate realization; four years to 50% effective adoption reflects implementation and workflow friction.

terminal_value.justification: The code still contains paid representation relationships, but programmatic and digital migration can bypass or compress the intermediary commission base.

cross_checks.no_proxy_confirmed: No. The role mix is a 4-digit 5418 proxy; BTOS and valuation evidence are broader proxies, all disclosed on affected inputs.
cross_checks.pricing_model_deflation: Commission-on-sales pricing initially retains labor savings, but media-owner in-housing, rate renegotiation and programmatic routing create material commission-rate and volume deflation risk.

dataset_mismatch.description: The deterministic 4-digit 5418 occupation mix is materially broader than 541840 and includes agency/PR/creative occupations not literal to a media representative.
sensitivity: Lower actual sales-support share would reduce V.
Higher traffic/billing share would increase V.

market_boundary.dominant_subsegment: Uncertain; narrative emphasizes the broadcast/linear-exposed incumbent evidence but does not assume it represents the entire code.
market_boundary.selection_basis: Census code definition plus available public incumbent disclosure; target diligence must identify its represented inventory mix.

top_uncertain_inputs.score_impact: A shifts from 5.0 at four years toward 3.3–5.0 across the stated range.
Capture score is highly sensitive to contract transferability and represented-media-owner bypass.
M can turn negative/no-upside if a buyer cannot acquire transferable commission contracts at a discount.

reviewer_note: All six score-driving source URLs were reopened on 2026-07-21. Exact 541840 owner-age, succession, independent-target and transaction data were not found in the reopened public sources; those inputs are deliberately low-confidence estimates rather than presented as observed facts.
